calls.title: Facilio Deyaar Refresher 03
calls.summary: Deyaar refresher #3 walked the Burlington Tower team through Facilio Workstation/Vendor apps, mobile work order flows and PPM/reactive distinctions. Adoption-lift exercise; no escalations.
key_points: Walkthrough of Facilio Workstation and Vendor apps for work order management, asset tracking and inspections
Confirmed all 68 properties and their assets have been digitized into Facilio
Demonstrated mobile work order creation, PPM vs reactive flows, and material proposal threshold
Discussed Dynamics integration as a future possibility; users instructed to focus on Facilio for now
Covered incident reporting and inspection workflows, flagging permission/automation areas
decisions: Users to continue using Facilio for current operations; Dynamics integration deferred
Refresher training to continue to lift active usage

action_items.task: Continue Deyaar refresher series; share recording / docs

risks: Adoption risk if active engagement and feedback don't pick up; potential confusion if users default to Dynamics for live tasks.
open_items: User feedback on permission tweaks and incident automation.

calls.title: Good Hands — Admin Training
calls.summary: Admin training on state flows, approvals and custom buttons for Good Hands. Customer engaged, no blockers — guidance to build new flows rather than edit existing ones.
key_points: Niviya walked through state flow configuration — adding states, transitions, conditions
Prashanth asked about editing existing flows and creating custom workflows per category
Paurnika clarified that editing existing flows is discouraged; create new flows for new categories
Reviewed approval process configuration: multiple approvers, approve/reject actions, resend permissions
Covered custom buttons that transition state across modules (e.g., WO approval → inventory request)
decisions: Customer to create category-specific state flows rather than editing live ones
Custom buttons enabled for cross-module state transitions where needed

action_items.task: Share configuration recording and quick-reference docs
Try state flow build in sandbox before applying to production

risks: Risk of breaking live flows if existing state flows are edited rather than cloned.
open_items: Customer sandbox practice before applying to production.

calls.title: Daily Facilio CAFM Workshops | Resolving Issues
calls.summary: Daily CAFM workshop did not run — CIT attendees were in another meeting. Call closed after ~35 seconds with a plan to reschedule.
key_points: Call ended after ~35 seconds — key CIT attendees were occupied in another meeting
Dinesh asked to be pinged once Vinay was free so workshop could resume
decisions: Workshop effectively deferred; CIT to ping team when available

calls.title: ST — Report & Dashboard Training
participants: Arun Kumar V (Facilio); Marshed Sattam Al Hathal
calls.summary: Report/dashboard training for Saudi Tabreed PPM & CM SLAs. Customer engaged but wanted more depth — explicit follow-up scheduled. Backend has a real PPM due-date display gap to chase down.
key_points: Walkthrough of report & dashboard configuration for PPM and CM work orders
Reviewed SLA compliance metrics — response, attendance, fix times
Identified gaps in populating/displaying due dates for PPM WOs — needs backend investigation
Discussed regional dashboard filtering; interim workaround = add region abbreviations to site names
Customer asked for consolidated view of upcoming + current month work orders
decisions: Interim: encode region into site names for filtering
Backend team to investigate PPM due-date display issue
Schedule follow-up training to close remaining gaps

action_items.task: Investigate PPM due-date population on backend
Schedule next training session with Marshed

risks: PPM due-date display gap; manual workaround for regional filtering increases admin overhead.
open_items: Backend investigation on PPM due dates; customer site naming convention update.

calls.title: ENEC — BRD Scope Alignment | Office Space Management
calls.summary: ENEC scope alignment on mail, parking and office space management. Customer pressed for firm timeline — Ashwin committed to revert EOD.
key_points: Walked mail management workflows HQ→site, site→HQ, external mail handling — confirmed
Mapped current manual parking-request process: forms, approvals, manual parking-layout updates
Discussed user-portal access for parking-request tracking
Explored iServices and HR system integration for seamless data flow
Touched office space management, meeting-room analytics, user-driven layout edits
decisions: Unified parking system desired — form, approval and layout update integrated
Auto-update parking layout on slot allocation, no manual intervention
Mail management workflows validated as-is

action_items.task: Revert with consolidated BRD timeline by end of day
Confirm iServices/HR integration details

risks: Tight BRD timeline; user-driven office layout edits feasibility unclear; integration scope with iServices/HR not yet sized.
open_items: BRD timeline confirmation; iServices/HR integration scope from ENEC.

calls.title: Edarat — Workshop 2 | Permit to Work
calls.summary: Productive PTW workshop with Edarat — confirmed multi-type PTW per WO, vendor vs in-house flow, and safety officer review path.
key_points: Refining PTW process for high-risk activities in the CAFM system
Discussed integration of RAMS and SOPs/MOPs with PTW; vendor vs in-house generation flow
Multiple PTW types per WO with type-specific prerequisites approved
Confirmed safety officer's role in PTW review and approval
Defined approval workflow for vendor-submitted PTWs
decisions: Allow multiple PTW types per work order with distinct prerequisites
Vendor PTWs follow defined approval workflow; in-house generated PTWs handled separately
Safety officer confirmed as PTW reviewer

action_items.task: Finalize PTW workflow design and share for sign-off

risks: Complexity of multi-type PTW prerequisites; ensuring SOP/MOP attachments stay in sync.
open_items: Customer sign-off on finalized PTW design.

calls.title: Deyaar Refresher Training 04
calls.summary: Fourth Deyaar refresher — Workstation app, WO lifecycle, PPM scheduling. Adoption-lift exercise, nothing material to flag.
key_points: Re-established Facilio Workstation as the primary tool for TFM vendors, engineers, technicians
Walked building structure, asset hierarchy, maintenance task setup
Detailed WO lifecycle from creation through closure — technician assignment, materials, proposals
Reinforced PPM scheduling and asset-linked WO routing
decisions: Workstation mobile app confirmed as primary tool

action_items.task: Continue refresher cadence; collect usage telemetry

risks: Sustained adoption if users default back to old systems.
open_items: Telemetry on active usage post-refresher series.

calls.title: Daylesford UAT — Reactive Flow
calls.summary: Daylesford reactive flow UAT — full lifecycle demo, mandatory-attachment feedback captured, non-Facilio vendor handling discussed. Healthy UAT progression.
key_points: End-to-end reactive flow demo: service request → WO → internal tech / external vendor → resolution
Internal tech flow includes dynamic RAMs and SLA tracking
External vendor flow covers 'require support' and notification channels
Mandatory attachment on WO resolution surfaced as UAT feedback
Discussed non-Facilio vendor handling and approval routing
decisions: Site manager approval sufficient for most WOs; finance software handles major approvals
Add mandatory attachment requirement on WO resolution as enhancement
Schedule ad-hoc data onboarding call for upcoming dependencies

action_items.task: Implement mandatory attachment on WO resolution per UAT feedback
Set up data onboarding call for dependent modules

risks: Non-Facilio vendor handling adds complexity to notification routing; UAT coverage for planned and reactive flows still in progress.
open_items: Data onboarding for dependent modules; UAT signoff for reactive flow.

calls.title: THG Project Kickoff
calls.summary: THG implementation kickoff. Scope, methodology and governance aligned with Oliver Scarf as POC. Customer pressing for ASAP go-live; revised plan being issued, with stakeholder availability and data delays flagged as the main slippage levers.
key_points: Kickoff to align scope, methodology and timeline for Facilio implementation at THG
Oliver Scarf designated primary POC for THG
Implementation methodology: kickoff → requirement gathering → solution alignment → BRD sign-off → configuration → UAT → go-live
Scope: portfolio, assets, service requests, planned maintenance, vendor portals
Workshop dates tentatively set for following week; revised plan to follow
Governance: weekly status updates and monthly engagement reviews
decisions: Oliver Scarf = primary THG POC
Phased methodology accepted; weekly status + monthly reviews adopted
Workshop slots tentatively next week, subject to revision

action_items.task: Share project documentation and data onboarding templates
Send revised project plan to THG
Confirm workshop attendance and review templates

risks: Stakeholder unavailability, data delays, decision-making lag pushing timeline. Mitigations: weekly governance, structured change management, shared ownership framework.
open_items: Documentation/template sharing; workshop attendance confirmation; revised plan delivery.

calls.title: Facilio Project Update Recurring | Imperial Cleaning
calls.summary: Imperial Cleaning weekly update. Phase one is largely on track but is being held by missing Canadian region/district mapping for 26 sites — Facilio recommending a geographical API. Multiple parallel workstreams (SLA billing, schedule change, assignment rules) progressing.
key_points: Walked status of vendor rejection reasons, invoice/credit-note netting, geolocation mobile reqs, recurring service frequencies
Regional scoping for vendor onboarding — Canadian region/district mapping missing, geo API recommended for address validation
GL code integration with BC pending Sergio's documentation (expected next day)
Service level billing implemented and in sandbox testing — detailed test case report to follow
Rejected invoice UX update — 'Create Invoice' button on header with linked rejected invoice ID
Auto timesheet for vendors being considered (adoption-positive, risks noted)
Schedule change functionality near completion; assignment-rules + conflict resolution combined
decisions: Phase-one items prioritized before any new scope items
Use geo API for address validation as path forward on Canadian mapping
Net invoice/credit notes via combined pre-bill + post-credit-note logic

action_items.task: Deliver service-level billing test case report
Resolve Canadian region/district mapping via geo API
Share GL code documentation with BC integration team
Demo combined assignment-rules + conflict resolution model
Confirm RFQ flow scope in next session

risks: Canadian region/district mapping blocker; address-integration scope creep; GL code documentation dependency on Sergio.
open_items: Canadian zip-code mapping for 26 sites; Sergio's GL code docs; sandbox test case report.

calls.title: Woodman's Slow Leak Detection — Touchbase
calls.summary: Woodman's slow-leak touchbase. Clarified that 16 stores are connected but only 12 have liquid-level sensors; CO2 racks lack sensors and will limit leak detection. Stores 1 and 37 onboarding to close before next call.
key_points: Clarified rack configurations and data availability across multiple sites
16 stores connected (vs initially assumed 12); only 12 have liquid-level sensors — aligns with project scope
Racks LT B, LT J, AC22, PRP confirmed not micro-thermal; may lack liquid-level indicators
CO2 racks (I-rack, G-rack in Beloit) lack liquid-level sensors — affects leak-detection algorithms
Agent install, controller discovery, point mapping complete for connected stores
Stores 1 and 37 onboarding targeted for completion before next call; raw alarms request
decisions: Proceed with leak detection for the 12 stores with liquid-level sensors
Enable raw alarms for pilot stores
Complete onboarding of stores 1 and 37 before next touchbase

action_items.task: Complete onboarding of stores 1 and 37
Enable raw alarm functionality for pilot stores
Document rack configuration assumptions and gaps for customer

risks: Leak-detection algorithm coverage gap on CO2 racks without sensors; expectation mismatch on connected-store count if scope is interpreted as all 16.
open_items: Sensor coverage on CO2 racks; completion of stores 1 and 37 onboarding.

Upcoming Fifth Third Bank/Burlington-area merger context surfaced
decisions: Next step: scoped follow-up demo with broader Fifth Third Bank stakeholders

calls.title: Facilio <-> MM — Catchup
calls.summary: Metro Maintenance catchup: re-implementation has clearly worked and customer is happy — but KK is being moved off the dedicated night shift on Monday due to health, with a one-month gradual handover to a trained support team plus bi-weekly catch-ups to monitor.

KK ending dedicated night-shift support by Monday due to health concerns from the night shift
Dedicated support team being trained and equipped with internal tool to take over ongoing support
KK to remain available for ad-hoc and critical issue resolution; gradual handover over next month
Dedicated email ID to be set up for urgent queries and KK contact
Bi-weekly catch-ups scheduled for the next two months to monitor transition
Jira to continue as the issue reporting channel; support team will have access
decisions: KK dedicated night-shift support ends Monday
Support team takes ongoing coverage; KK available on call for critical issues
Bi-weekly catch-ups for next two months
Dedicated email ID for urgent / KK-direct queries

Confirm gradual handover plan for KK over next month
Route urgent items to new email ID; continue Jira for tracked issues

Recurring issue: refrigerant cylinder tracking not well integrated into work orders, causing technician confusion and compliance risk
Performance issues raised on action buttons and admin overrides — partially attributed to internet and background scripts
Vendor contact management — clarified deletion of incorrect entries and deactivation of users
Training materials for refrigerant compliance and vendor management shared and to be added to dashboards
New store 47 launch scheduled for October 1; potential on-site visit for remote monitoring setup
WOs stuck in 'waiting compliance event' status to be resolved
decisions: Postpone asset list import for store 47 until updated data is available
Enhance refrigerant cylinder data capture and link to WOs for compliance + usability
Add training materials to dashboards
Plan possible on-site visit for store 47 remote monitoring setup

Design refrigerant cylinder → WO integration enhancement
Coordinate store 47 data and Oct 1 launch readiness
Provide updated store 47 data when ready
